bullets.text: A worldwide effort is underway to trace passengers from the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius, with at least five confirmed cases and several countries linked to the outbreak. Two Britons evacuated from the ship are reportedly improving in hospital.

Tensions between the US and Iran continue, with Iran accusing the US of "reckless military adventure" after an exchange of fire in the Hormuz Strait, despite a reported ceasefire. The US also seized enriched uranium from Venezuela, citing frustration with Iran.
An international cyber attack has disrupted numerous universities and schools globally after a hacking group breached Canvas, a widely used academic software.
A Royal Commission in Australia has begun investigating antisemitism and social cohesion, with early testimonies highlighting fear and safety concerns among anonymous witnesses.
US President Donald Trump announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, effective from May 9 to 11, via social media.
The son of Aung San Suu Kyi is demanding proof of his mother's well-being, as Myanmar's military junta continues to detain tens of thousands of political prisoners.
US President Donald Trump has changed his approach in the war against Iran, with a focus on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, leaving nuclear and ballistic missile negotiations for later.

The Federal Reserve states that stability risks from further private credit redemption requests appear "limited and manageable," even after some major market players blocked investors from accessing funds.
Reform has seen a surge in England, while nationalist parties have triumphed in Scotland and Wales, with Nigel Farage’s party securing over 1,100 seats.
